rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Celestine,

I know this has been a frustrating few weeks, and I really appreciate you sending all those photos and forwarding both order confirmations. I've gone through everything carefully so I can give you a clear answer.

For order R-8801, delivered April 18 and reported April 22, the cracked mixing bowl set falls within the 5-day damage window. I can process a partial refund of $29.40 for that set only. The silicone tray set and dish rack arrived undamaged per your photos, so they are not covered.

For order R-8854, delivered May 1 and reported May 3, the blender lid damage was reported outside the 5-day window. I can send one replacement blender lid at no cost as a goodwill gesture, but I cannot process a cash refund for it.

To move forward, please send your current shipping address by May 14 and confirm you're okay with the $29.40 partial refund for R-8801. Once I have both, I'll queue the refund and replacement. If the $29.40 doesn't appear within five business days after processing, please reach back out to me directly.

ForbiddenAbstained: Do not change the $59.00 credit amount or the $30.00 post-credit balance.
rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Bertrand,

I've reviewed your billing dispute and want to give you the full picture, as there are three invoices involved, each with a slightly different situation.

Invoice INV-2210 is for $148.00, covering March 1 through March 31. Your team was on the Standard plan for that whole month, and you downgraded on April 1. Since the service was used for the entire billing period with no mid-month changes, I cannot issue a refund for this invoice.

Invoice INV-2291 is also for $148.00, covering April 1 through April 30. Your downgrade was processed on April 1, but the system charged the Standard rate instead of the Basic rate of $89.00 because the plan switch did not propagate before the invoice ran. This is a billing error. I can issue a $59.00 credit—the $148.00 Standard charge minus the $89.00 Basic rate you should have been charged—to your next invoice, INV-2361, if you confirm by June 5.

Invoice INV-2361 is the current open invoice of $89.00 for May 1 through May 31, and it is correct. After applying the $59.00 credit, the balance on INV-2361 would be $30.00. I need your confirmation by June 5 to process the credit; otherwise, the credit window for INV-2291 closes and the full $89.00 on INV-2361 remains due.

Best,

ForbiddenAbstained: Do not include custom dashboard integration in any of the three options.
Do not change the seat price, the API flat fee, or the dedicated onboarding fee.
Do not extend the June 20 expiry date.
Do not offer additional work or scope that is not listed in the three options.
rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Ingrid,

Thank you for the time on Friday and for the detailed notes your team sent over the weekend. I want to make sure quote Q-5544 is easy to compare against the two add-on options I mentioned, so I am laying everything out in one email.

The base quote Q-5544 is 20 seats at $55 per seat per month, which includes the core platform, onboarding, and standard email support. This quote expires on June 20. The total at this tier is $1,100 per month.

Add-on A is the API access package. If your team adds API access, the seat price stays the same at $55 per seat per month but there is an additional flat fee of $300 per month for API access. That brings the monthly total to $1,400.

Add-on B is the dedicated onboarding program, which is a one-time fee of $1,800 and covers four structured onboarding sessions. The dedicated onboarding is not included in the base quote or Add-on A. If you add both API access and dedicated onboarding, the monthly recurring cost is $1,400 plus the one-time $1,800.

One thing I want to be clear about: the custom dashboard integration your team mentioned on Friday is not in any of these three options. That would require a separate scoping conversation and a separate quote. I cannot include it in Q-5544 or the add-ons without a new approval.

All three options expire on June 20. If you would like to go with one of the options as described, please reply by June 20 and I can send the order form the same day. If you need an extension or want to add the custom dashboard scope, let me know and I can start that process separately.

Best,

ForbiddenAbstained: Do not say students entering grade 3 in the fall are eligible.
Do not say partial payment is accepted or that a spot can be held without full payment.
Do not extend or soften the June 13 deadline.
rows.RewrittenText:
Hello Ridgemont families,

We’re excited to share details about the Summer Enrichment Program for the 2026 summer session. The program runs July 7 through July 25 on the Ridgemont East campus, Room 104, and is open to students currently enrolled in grades 3 through 6 at Ridgemont. Students entering grade 3 in the fall are not eligible—enrollment is for current grade 3 through grade 6 students only.

We can accept up to 24 students. If we receive more than 24 completed applications, students will be placed on a waitlist in the order applications are received. Being on the interest list from last year does not guarantee a spot—all families must submit a new application this year. The program fee is $185 per student, due with the application. We cannot accept partial payment or hold a spot without payment.

Applications and payment must reach the main office by Friday, June 13 at 4 p.m. The application form is Form SEP-2026, available at the main office window or on the school website. If a student is placed on the waitlist, we’ll hold the $185 until a spot opens or the deadline passes, then return it if no spot opens. Questions? Email enrichment@example.com or stop by the main office Tuesday through Thursday, 8 a.m. to 3 p.m.

Best,

I checked order ORD-29447 against the return you started on June 2. Each item is at a different stage, so here’s where things stand:

- Ceramic pour-over carafe (SKU CPV-11): The return label was emailed on June 3. We haven’t received the package yet. Once it arrives, I’ll process a $34.00 refund to your card within 3–5 business days.
- Stainless travel cup (SKU STC-07): The package arrived on June 5 and we confirmed the item is undamaged and unused. The $22.50 refund was issued on June 6 and should appear on your statement within 2 business days.
- Replacement lid (SKU LID-04): You noted the lid was cracked on arrival. I need one photo of the cracked lid before I can send a replacement. Please send the photo by June 10 at noon or the replacement request will close automatically.

I can’t issue a refund for the pour-over carafe until we physically receive the item, and I can’t send the replacement lid before the photo arrives. Everything else is in progress.

Best,

ForbiddenAbstained: Do not interpret or describe the contents of the imaging records.
Do not say the release has already been processed or is in progress.
Do not accept a paper form photo as an alternative to the portal submission.
rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Gwendolyn,

I'm following up on your request to send your imaging records from your April 18 appointment to Dr. Okafor's office. We can't release records without your signed authorization, so here's what to do:

1. Log in to the patient portal at portal.example.org and go to "My Records" > "Release Authorization."
2. Complete the Release of Medical Records form. Select Dr. Okafor's practice as the recipient and check "Diagnostic Imaging" under record type.
3. Sign and submit the form inside the portal—the portal version is the only one our records team accepts. Please don't email a photo of a paper form.

Once we receive the signed form, our records team typically processes release requests within 5 business days. If you haven't heard anything within 7 business days of submitting, call the records office at ext. 3114.

I can't give you a status or contact Dr. Okafor's office until the signed authorization is in our system. If this is urgent, please ask Dr. Okafor's office to mark the incoming request as urgent and fax a priority cover sheet to 555-0192.

Best,

ForbiddenAbstained: Do not diagnose or label Soren behaviorally.
Do not say the check-in with Ms. Vega is disciplinary.
Do not blame Ingrid or suggest parenting issues.
rows.RewrittenText:
Dear Ingrid,

On May 15 during independent reading, Soren pushed another student's book off the desk. When I asked him to return to his seat, he said he didn't care and turned away. I'm sharing what I saw so we can figure out together what support looks like going forward—I'm not labeling Soren or suggesting there's something wrong with him.

Since May 15 I've checked in with Soren every morning at arrival, and he's been calm and engaged. I've also put his name on Ms. Vega's list for a voluntary check-in. Ms. Vega is our school counselor who works with students in these situations, and this is not a disciplinary step.

Can we meet to talk this through? I'm free May 22 after 3:30 p.m. or May 24 between 9 and 10 a.m. Let me know which works, and we'll confirm by end of day May 21.

Best,

rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Octavia,

Thank you for the call on May 16 and for sharing the feature request summary. I want to be transparent about where things stand.

The two features you outlined—bulk role assignment via CSV and a dedicated audit log export API—are on our product roadmap, but they are not committed to a specific release date. I cannot tell you they will be ready by your contract renewal on September 1 because our product team has not confirmed a timeline yet. If either feature lands before renewal, I will notify you immediately.

Your account ID KG-00412 runs through August 31, and the renewal at the current rate is $3,400 per month for the 40-seat Enterprise plan. To make sure your use case is documented, I submitted a formal feature priority request on May 17—reference FPR-2241. I can also connect you with our enterprise support team for a more detailed roadmap conversation. If you would like that, please reply and I will set up an intro by the end of this week.

I just cannot make a commitment that custom development or a discounted renewal will happen to close this gap.

Best,
